negative_control.protection: Postgres RLS fail-closed (NN-1: missing GUC → NULL → 0 rows)
negative_control.command: docker exec brainv3-postgres-1 psql -U brain_app -d brain -c "SELECT count(*) as no_guc_result FROM bronze_events WHERE event_id = 'dd000099-0000-4000-8000-000000000099';"
negative_control.outcome: RED — no GUC returns 0 rows confirming fail-closed behavior

negative_control.protection: Superuser bypass — brain superuser sees rows regardless (proves test must run as brain_app)
negative_control.command: docker exec brainv3-postgres-1 psql -U brain -d brain -c "SELECT current_user, count(*) FROM bronze_events WHERE event_id = 'dd000099-0000-4000-8000-000000000099' GROUP BY current_user;"
negative_control.outcome: PROOF that superuser bypasses RLS — if test ran as 'brain' it would see the row regardless of GUC (false-pass trap, F-4)

negative_control.outcome: GREEN — correct brand GUC returns 1 row (RLS permits own-brand access)
